expectations.evidence: Called get_customers with search='Elicio', returned 2 results (Elicio + elicio test), correctly identified the main one.
Called get_contracts with customer_id=90ecb210-dd6b-4cfb-aac7-83e175388115 and asset_uuid=7de54b47-09c8-4cd6-8f6e-a444d7b4706d (Arendonk). Used the asset_uuid filter directly.
Would display 3 contracts in a readable format. Data is clean and structured.
Found 3 contracts: Injection SPOT Contract (dynamic, sell), Consumption SPOT Contract (dynamic, buy), Imbalance (imbalance, bidirectional). All on the Arendonk asset.

issue_found: The skill doesn't explain how to resolve an asset name to UUID when get_all_assets is unavailable (broken on remote MCP). In this eval, I already knew the UUID from the evals.json context. A real session would need to either: (a) use get_contracts without filter and scan results, or (b) use the filter_options in the contracts response to find asset UUIDs. The skill should address this fallback path.
